usage_notes: Masculine noun; can also refer to tense in grammar
common_collocations: quel temps, beau temps, mauvais temps
image_context: A scenic view of sunny weather with blue sky and clouds
example_1_translation: What is the weather like today?
example_2: Le temps est beau ce matin.
example_2_translation: The weather is nice this morning.
memory_hook: Temps = time and weather - they both change constantly
related_words: la météo, le climat, la saison
common_mistakes: Remember 'temps' is masculine (le temps), not feminine

usage_notes: Uncountable noun; use 'goutte de pluie' for drops
common_collocations: sous la pluie, dans la pluie, la pluie tombe
image_context: Rain drops falling on a puddle reflecting city lights
example_2: J'ai oublié mon parapluie, la pluie est arrivée.
example_2_translation: I forgot my umbrella, the rain arrived.
example_3: La pluie rend le sol mouillé.
example_3_translation: The rain makes the ground wet.
memory_hook: Pluie sounds like 'plooh-ee' - the sound of rain falling
common_mistakes: Pluie is feminine (la pluie), use 'la' not 'le'

usage_notes: Masculine noun; rarely pluralized unless poetic
common_collocations: le soleil brille, au soleil, sous le soleil
image_context: A bright golden sun with rays shining in a blue sky
example_1: Le soleil brille fort en été.
example_1_translation: The sun shines brightly in summer.
example_2: Je protège mes yeux du soleil.
example_2_translation: I protect my eyes from the sun.
example_3_translation: In the sun, we are hot.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'so-lay'; the 'l' at the end is pronounced
memory_hook: Soleil = sol (ground) + eil (eye) - the eye of the earth
related_words: le jour, l'été, la chaleur, l'été
common_mistakes: Don't confuse soleil (sun) with sole (sole fish)

usage_notes: Feminine noun; formal or casual dresses
common_collocations: une belle robe, porter une robe, la robe rouge
image_context: An elegant red dress on a mannequin in a boutique
example_1: Elle porte une belle robe rouge.
example_1_translation: She is wearing a beautiful red dress.
example_3_translation: I like this dress very much.
common_mistakes: Robe is feminine (la robe), not masculine

usage_notes: Plural form; singular is 'une chaussure' (one shoe)
common_collocations: des chaussures neuves, enlever les chaussures, mettre les chaussures
image_context: A pair of stylish brown leather shoes on a wooden floor
example_2: Enlève tes chaussures à la porte.
example_2_translation: Take off your shoes at the door.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'sho-sur'; the 'ch' makes a 'sh' sound
memory_hook: Chaussures = show your feet with shoes
related_words: le pied, le soulier, la botte
common_mistakes: Usually used in plural; singular form is 'une chaussure'

common_collocations: mettre le manteau, enlever le manteau, un manteau chaud
image_context: A warm wool coat hanging on a coat rack in winter
example_1: Je mets mon manteau parce qu'il fait froid.
example_1_translation: I put on my coat because it is cold.
example_2: Le manteau est trop grand pour moi.
example_2_translation: The coat is too big for me.
example_3: Son manteau noir est très élégant.
example_3_translation: His black coat is very elegant.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'mon-toe'; the 't' is not silent
memory_hook: Manteau = man-tow, a coat covers you like a manual cape
related_words: l'hiver, le froid, l'habit, la veste
common_mistakes: Manteau is masculine (le manteau), not feminine

common_collocations: une pomme rouge, manger une pomme, une pomme verte
image_context: A shiny red apple with a green leaf on a white surface
example_1: Je mange une pomme chaque matin.
example_1_translation: I eat an apple every morning.
example_2: La pomme est rouge et juteuse.
example_2_translation: The apple is red and juicy.
example_3: Une pomme par jour, c'est bon pour la santé.
example_3_translation: An apple a day is good for health.
memory_hook: Pomme = po-mm, sounds like pomp, an apple is a posh fruit
related_words: le fruit, l'arbre, la santé, le verger
common_mistakes: Pomme is feminine (la pomme), not masculine

common_collocations: une carotte orange, manger une carotte, de la carotte râpée
image_context: Fresh orange carrots with green tops on a market table
example_1: Le lapin mange beaucoup de carottes.
example_1_translation: The rabbit eats a lot of carrots.
example_2: Je prépare une soupe à la carotte.
example_3: Les carottes sont bonnes pour la vue.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'kar-ot'; ends with a 't' sound
memory_hook: Carotte = car-ote, a carrot is a root crop
related_words: le légume, l'orange, le lapin, le jardin
common_mistakes: Carotte is feminine (la carotte), not masculine

common_collocations: une banane jaune, manger une banane, de la banane
image_context: A bunch of ripe yellow bananas on a kitchen counter
example_1: La banane est mon fruit préféré.
example_1_translation: The banana is my favorite fruit.
example_2: Je prends une banane pour le petit déjeuner.
example_2_translation: I have a banana for breakfast.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'ba-nan'; similar to English but with French 'a' sound
memory_hook: Banane = ba-nan, bananas go na-na (crazy yellow)
related_words: le fruit, le petit déjeuner, l'école, la grappe
common_mistakes: Banane is feminine (la banane), not masculine

usage_notes: Feminine noun; parent who gave birth
common_collocations: ma mère, la mère de, maman
image_context: A smiling mother hugging her child in a kitchen
example_2_translation: I call my mother every week.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'mer'; the 'r' is rolled in French
memory_hook: Mère = mother, sounds like 'mare' - the female horse
related_words: maman, le père, l'enfant, la famille
common_mistakes: Mère is feminine (la mère); don't confuse with mer (sea)

common_collocations: mon père, le père de, papa
image_context: A father teaching his son to ride a bicycle in a park
example_2: Le père et le fils jouent ensemble.
example_2_translation: The father and son play together.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'pair'; similar to the English word 'pair'
memory_hook: Père = pair/pear, a father is the pair of the mother
related_words: papa, la mère, l'enfant, la famille
common_mistakes: Père is masculine (le père); don't confuse with pair (pair)

common_collocations: ma sœur, la sœur de, grande sœur, petite sœur
image_context: Two sisters walking together and laughing in autumn
example_1: Ma sœur est plus âgée que moi.
example_1_translation: My sister is older than me.
example_2: Je joue avec ma sœur le week-end.
example_2_translation: I play with my sister on the weekend.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'ser'; the 'œ' is like the 'u' in English 'put'
memory_hook: Sœur = sister, sounds like 'sur' (on) - your sister is on your side
related_words: le frère, l'enfant, la famille, le frère
common_mistakes: Sœur is feminine (la sœur); don't confuse with frère (brother)

usage_notes: Adjective that agrees with gender and number; use 'être heureux'
common_collocations: très heureux, tellement heureux, heureux de
image_context: A person with a big smile and sparkling eyes full of joy
example_1: Je suis heureux de te voir.
example_1_translation: I am happy to see you.
memory_hook: Heureux = happy hour in French is when you're heureux
related_words: la joie, le sourire, le bonheur, joyeux
common_mistakes: Must agree with gender: heureux (masc), heureuse (fem)

usage_notes: Adjective that doesn't change with gender (same form)
common_collocations: très triste, un peu triste, triste de
image_context: A person sitting alone with head in hands, looking contemplative
example_1: Je suis triste quand il pleut.
example_1_translation: I am sad when it rains.
example_2: Le film était triste mais beau.
example_2_translation: The film was sad but beautiful.
memory_hook: Triste = try to smile, but triste won't let you
related_words: la tristesse, pleurer, la peine, malheureux
common_mistakes: Triste stays the same for all genders in singular

usage_notes: Masculine noun; tool for driving nails
common_collocations: un marteau lourd, cogner avec le marteau, le manche du marteau
image_context: A sturdy metal hammer resting on a wooden workbench
example_1: J'utilise le marteau pour planter le clou.
example_1_translation: I use the hammer to drive in the nail.
example_2: Le marteau est dans la boîte à outils.
example_2_translation: The hammer is in the toolbox.
pronunciation_tips: Sounds like 'mar-toe'; the final 'u' is silent
memory_hook: Marteau = mar-toe, sounds like a hammer hitting something
related_words: l'outil, le clou, la boîte, la construction
common_mistakes: Marteau is masculine (le marteau), not feminine
